steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The opening condition “molecular analysis does not require distinguishing subtle physiological differences” is the decisive clue.

steps.title: Match the advantage to the choices
steps.content:
This means molecular analysis can identify morphologically similar but distinct species—something morphological identification cannot do.

Choice A correctly states that molecular is therefore better than the morphological method (used for Prionospio branchilucida) at distinguishing similar-looking species.

The other choices misidentify the advantage:
  • B wrongly emphasizes usage frequency
  • C contradicts the passage by suggesting molecular is better for the species where it was impracticable
  • D falsely claims molecular provides physiological detail when it doesn't require it
.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
